A Hidden Gem in the Monadnock Region

Welcome home to the Meadmere.

A historic four-room bed & breakfast nestled on 10.5 quiet acres, five miles from downtown Keene, New Hampshire.

Check Availability → Ask about our Romantic Weekend for Two
Scroll
I About the Inn

A quiet rural setting, a long story to tell.

The Tartan Fox is the Keene/Swanzey area's hidden gem — a historic Meadmere Manor home on 10.5 acres, just five miles from downtown Keene. Newcomers leave raving. Returning guests come back, again and again.

Begin your stay with a refreshing night's sleep in one of our four beautifully decorated rooms. Wake to a three-course gourmet breakfast. Spend the day exploring covered bridges, antique shops, hiking trails, and Keene's art fairs. Return to a cozy evening at the Inn — a book in the library, the piano in the corner, the quiet of the woods just outside.

Your stay with us begins before you ever get here. — The Innkeepers
4 Beautifully Appointed Rooms
10.5 Wooded & Garden Acres
III Course Gourmet Breakfast
II The Rooms

Four rooms, four companions.

Each room is named for a Scottish fox who keeps watch over it. Every room has a queen bed, a private marble-floored bath (heated in winter), and the kind of quiet only ten and a half acres can give you.

Meet Mac
No. 01 — Mac

The Manor Room

Bay windows overlook the gardens. Antique woodwork, a queen bed dressed in heirloom quilting, and a private marble-floored bath in the classic Meadmere style.

View Room →
Meet Angus
No. 02 — Angus

The Blackwatch Room

Black Watch tartan dresses every window. Hardwood floors, a brick chimney corner, and the warm hush you only get from old New England farmhouses.

View Room →
Meet Gille
No. 03 — Gille

The Stewart Room

Named for Clan Stewart and dressed in its colors. A queen bed, a reading nook, and books in the room — a thoughtful pause from whatever the day held.

View Room →
Meet Niall
No. 04 — Niall

The Terrace Room

Tucked away with views over the property. Spacious, private, and bright in the morning — perfect for guests who want to slow down for a day or two.

View Room →
III In the Morning

Three courses. One long table.

Mornings here begin slowly. The breakfast room is panelled in old wood and seats eight comfortably — guests, conversation, and coffee in good company. Each plate is plated by hand, in three quiet courses.

I Fruit Seasonal melon, berries — bright, simple, and just enough.
II From the Oven Fresh-baked scones, dusted and warm from the kitchen.
III The Main Eggs Benedict, blueberry pancakes, bacon — whatever the morning calls for.
Summer afternoon, on property.
IV The Region

The Monadnock, all around you.

01

Mt. Monadnock

One of the most-climbed peaks in the world. Hike it in a morning and be back in time for tea on the porch.

~ 25 minutes by car
02

Historic Covered Bridges

Swanzey is home to four nineteenth-century covered bridges. A perfect afternoon's tour through quiet country roads.

~ 5–15 minutes from the inn
03

Keene Art & Antiquing

Galleries, art fairs, and the kind of antique shops you can spend a whole day in. Keene's downtown is just five miles north.

~ 10 minutes by car
04

Cross-Country & Downhill Skiing

Local resorts in winter, miles of cross-country trails the rest of the season. The Inn's fireplace waits for you when you return.

Open in season
05

Brattleboro & Beyond

Cross the river into Vermont. Farmer's markets, riverside walks, and small towns that feel a hundred miles from anywhere.

~ 25 minutes by car
Look for the Sign

"At the edge of the woods, hand-painted on a green ground — a small fox at the window, watching for you."

Drive past it once, and you'll always know how to find your way back.

V A Few Glimpses

The Inn, in fragments.
